name: AUK
tagline: The API client that talks MCP back

hero.headline: The API client that talks MCP back
hero.subhead: AUK is a native, keyboard-first API client with k6 load testing, git-native YAML storage, and MCP built in both directions — an embedded MCP server so Claude Code can drive the app, and an MCP client debugger to inspect any MCP server's tools.

pillars.title: Embedded MCP server
pillars.description: Toggle it on and AUK exposes list_workspaces, list_requests, run_request, and run_perf_test over Streamable HTTP on a loopback port with a bearer token — so Claude Code can drive the app directly. Mutating requests still pop an in-app Allow/Deny prompt first.

pillars.title: MCP client debugger
pillars.description: The mirror image of the server: connect to any MCP server over stdio or Streamable HTTP, browse its published tools, and test-invoke them with a JSON-Schema-driven form — like the official MCP Inspector, integrated into AUK.

pillars.title: k6 load testing, built in
pillars.description: Turn any saved request into a load test — executor, virtual users, duration, and SLA thresholds — without leaving the request you're editing. Results stream live (req/s, p95) and finish with a pass/fail verdict.

pillars.title: Git-native storage
pillars.description: Every workspace is plain YAML under ~/.auk/workspace — a real git repo the whole time. Diff it, PR-review it, or commit and push right from the Git panel without dropping to a terminal.
pillars.snippet: git commit -m "add staging env vars" && git push

secondaryFeatures: HTTP, WebSocket, SSE, GraphQL, and gRPC (unary, fully dynamic — no precompiled stubs)
Basic, Bearer, API Key, JWT, and OAuth2 (client credentials) auth
Import cURL, OpenAPI 3.x / Swagger 2.0, or a Postman Collection — auto-detected
Declarative assertions (status/time/header/JSON-path) enforced in GUI, CLI, and MCP alike
Sandboxed pre-request JavaScript — no filesystem, network, or process access
httptrace timing waterfall: DNS, connect, TLS, TTFB, redirects
Response diffing against the previous response, plus ⌘F search
System/Light/Dark theme with self-hosted Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts
Command palette (⌘K) for requests, history, import, theme, and settings
Headless CLI (apitool-cli) for CI smoke tests, using the same engine as the GUI
positioningQuote: Postman wants your account, Yaak quietly dropped its MCP server, Bruno stops at HTTP/GraphQL with no perf testing — AUK ships k6 load testing, a two-way MCP integration, and git-native YAML storage in one native macOS app, no login required.
installSteps: Open the .dmg and drag AUK to Applications
Launch AUK — the app is signed and notarized, so Gatekeeper opens it without a warning

items: HTTP, WebSocket, SSE, GraphQL, gRPC (unary, fully dynamic — no precompiled stubs)
Auth: Basic, Bearer, API Key, JWT, OAuth2 (client credentials)
Import: cURL, OpenAPI 3.x / Swagger 2.0 (JSON or YAML), Postman Collection v2.1 — auto-detected from pasted text
Templating: {{ uuid }}, {{ timestamp.unix / unixMillis / iso8601 }}, {{ hash.md5/sha1/sha256(...) }}, {{ encode.base64/base64url(...) }}
Environment variables and request chaining (reference a previous response's JSON by path)
Pre-request scripting: sandboxed JavaScript (no filesystem/network/process access), read ctx.request, call ctx.setHeader(name, value), 2-second execution timeout
Assertions: status / responseTime / header / body-JSON-path, with eq/neq/contains/matches/lt/gt/exists/notExists
Assertions enforced identically in the GUI, the CLI (non-zero exit on failure), and over MCP
k6 executor + virtual users + duration configuration, next to the request you're editing
Live req/s and p95 chart while the test runs
SLA thresholds with an authoritative pass/fail verdict
k6 run as an arm's-length CLI sidecar (not linked into the app binary — a deliberate boundary given k6's AGPLv3 license)
Embedded MCP server: Streamable HTTP, loopback + bearer token
Exposes list_workspaces, list_requests, run_request, run_perf_test
In-app Allow/Deny approval prompt gates mutating requests (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) triggered over MCP
MCP client debugger: connect to any MCP server (stdio subprocess or Streamable HTTP)
Browse a connected server's published tools and test-invoke them with a JSON-Schema-driven form
Session history of MCP tool calls, with raw result and structured content shown side by side
Every workspace stored as plain, git-friendly YAML in ~/.auk/workspace — diffable and PR-reviewable
Git panel: branch, dirty state, changed files
Commit, and commit + push (if a remote is configured), without leaving the app
Headless CLI: apitool-cli run <requestID> --workspace-dir=DIR [--env=ENVIRONMENT_ID] — same engine as the GUI, exit code driven by assertions
Command palette (⌘K): jump to a request, browse history, import, switch theme, open settings
Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘N new request, ⌘B browse, ⌘, settings
Response viewer: theme-aware JSON syntax highlighting, ⌘F search, diff against the previous response, headers tab, timing/redirect tab
Request debugger: httptrace-level timing waterfall (DNS, connect, TLS handshake, TTFB, content download), redirect chain capture
Theme: System/Light/Dark, semantic CSS-variable tokens throughout, persisted to ~/.auk/settings.yaml
Self-hosted Inter (UI) and JetBrains Mono (code) fonts bundled with the app — no network fetch, no OS font fallback
Native macOS app built on Wails v2 — not a browser tab

screenshots.title: Empty native macOS window
screenshots.caption: A native macOS app window titled AUK with traffic-light buttons and a left icon sidebar shows an empty Demo Workspace state with a centered hint reading ‘cmd+K to jump anywhere’ plus ‘cmd+N for a new request, cmd+B to browse.’

screenshots.title: Core request/response view
screenshots.caption: A GET request to jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1 shown in the light theme, with the sidebar rail and a pretty-printed JSON response body plus a 200 OK / 268ms / 509B status line.

screenshots.title: Dark theme workspace
screenshots.caption: The same layout in dark mode, showing the Assert tab with two green checkmarked assertions above the JSON response pane.

screenshots.title: Settings appearance panel
screenshots.caption: The Settings dialog’s Appearance section with a System/Light/Dark toggle, persisted to ~/.auk/settings.yaml.

screenshots.title: Request timing waterfall
screenshots.caption: The Timing tab shows a colored waterfall bar — DNS lookup, connecting, TLS handshake, TTFB, content download — with millisecond values, totaling 268ms.

screenshots.title: Passing request assertions
screenshots.caption: The Assert tab with two configured checks (status equals 200, body JSON path contains a value) and an ‘ASSERTIONS PASSED 2/2’ banner.

screenshots.title: Pre-request script editor
screenshots.caption: The Script tab open with a one-line pre-request script setting a custom header before the request is sent.

screenshots.title: k6 load test configuration
screenshots.caption: The Perf tab with executor, virtual users, and duration fields, plus SLA threshold rows for request duration and error rate.

screenshots.title: Live load test results
screenshots.caption: A live dual-line chart (req/s and p95) during a run, finishing with a PASSED banner and a full stats grid with both SLA thresholds checked.

screenshots.title: Git workspace changes panel
screenshots.caption: The Git panel on branch master showing changed files, a commit message box, and commit history — without leaving the app.

screenshots.title: Import request dialog
screenshots.caption: The Import modal accepting a pasted cURL command, OpenAPI/Swagger spec, or Postman collection — auto-detected.

screenshots.title: MCP server settings panel
screenshots.caption: A running MCP server at http://127.0.0.1:8724/mcp with a copyable ‘claude mcp add’ command and a Stop button.

screenshots.title: MCP client tool list
screenshots.caption: The MCP sidebar listing a connected server’s four published tools, ready to browse and test-invoke.

screenshots.title: MCP tool call result view
screenshots.caption: Calling list_workspaces shows the raw result and structured content side by side, with a log of recent calls this session.

screenshots.title: Command palette with actions
screenshots.caption: ⌘K opens a fuzzy command list — new request, browse history, import, theme, settings — reachable without the mouse.
